entries.role: Always-on 3.3 V linear regulator that sources the LPC_VCC rail to the System Controller before any other rails are enabled; if it fails, the entire board is dead.
typical_failure_modes: shorted output pulling LPC_VCC to GND (leaky die or downstream short)
open output leaving LPC_VCC absent — D11 stays dark, LPC UART unresponsive
thermally-degraded output — low-voltage LPC_VCC causes erratic System Controller behavior
entries.notes: Probe U14 output pin in diode-mode for short before replacing; if shorted, isolate U18 and other downstream loads to confirm the fault is in U14 and not the load. Measure LPC_VCC at J22 pin 15 (nominal 3.3 V). Source: MNT Reform Operator Handbook (3rd Ed.) advanced.html

entries.role: NXP LPC11U24 Cortex-M0 System Controller MCU that sequences all power rails via GPIO, monitors LiFePO4 cell voltages over SPI, and accepts UART power-on/off commands.
typical_failure_modes: GPIO output damage from reversed-battery or overvoltage — rail-enable lines stuck low
SPI bus corruption preventing battery cell data readback from LTC6803
no UART response when LPC_VCC (3.3 V at J22 pin 15) is absent or low
entries.notes: Confirm LPC_VCC = 3.3 V at J22 pin 15 before suspecting U18 itself. Send `1P` command over UART to test power sequencing; watch D11/D12 LEDs for rail activity. Source: community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970

entries.role: Texas Instruments TUSB8041 4-port USB 3.0 hub that expands the CPU SOM's USB controllers to five total ports; requires 1.2 V (VCC_1V2) to operate.
typical_failure_modes: no USB enumeration on all ports when VCC_1V2 at C37 is absent
partial port failure if VCC_1V2 is marginal (under-voltage)
entries.notes: Probe C37 for 1.2 V before suspecting U9 die failure. If VCC_1V2 is present but USB is still dead, diode-mode probe decoupling caps on U9 supply pins for hidden short. Source: MNT Reform Operator Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Texas Instruments SN65DSI86 MIPI-DSI-to-eDP bridge IC that converts DSI signals from the CPU SOM to eDP for the internal display panel; requires VCC_1V2 and 3V3_PWR_AUX.
typical_failure_modes: dead internal display when VCC_1V2 (C37) is absent
cold joint causing intermittent or blank display — external HDMI unaffected
display flicker after reassembly due to bad J24 ZIF contact (not a die failure)
entries.notes: Reflow U10 with flux and hot-air if cold joint suspected; inspect under stereo microscope. Confirm VCC_1V2 at C37 and 3V3_PWR_AUX before replacing. Also inspect J24 (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) ZIF pads for lifted contacts. Source: community.mnt.re/t/edp-cable-or-board-issue/3898; MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Mini-PCIe slot connector for the i.MX8M primary PCIe controller, primarily used for a Wi-Fi card; power (PCIE1_PWR) is switched by the System Controller U18.
typical_failure_modes: Wi-Fi card not detected if PCIE1_PWR_EN GPIO from U18 is not asserted
intermittent card seating due to worn connector contacts
entries.notes: Verify System Controller has enabled PCIE1_PWR_EN before suspecting slot or card fault. Source: MNT Reform Handbook hardware.html

entries.role: Voltage level shifter for the SD card interface translating between 3.3 V and 1.8 V logic; non-functional if VCC_1V8 (C130) is absent.
typical_failure_modes: SD card boot failure when VCC_1V8 at C130 is absent — level shifter blocked
partial signal corruption if 1.8 V is marginal
entries.notes: Probe C130 for 1.8 V before suspecting U28. If 1.8 V is present but SD still fails, diode-mode probe U28 supply pins. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Voltage level shifter for the SD card interface translating between 3.3 V and 1.8 V logic; non-functional if VCC_1V8 (C130) is absent.
typical_failure_modes: SD card boot failure when VCC_1V8 at C130 is absent — level shifter blocked
partial signal corruption if 1.8 V is marginal
entries.notes: Works in tandem with U28; probe C130 for 1.8 V first. If rail is present but SD still fails, check U8 supply pins in diode-mode for short. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Analog Devices LTC4020 buck/boost regulator and LiFePO4 charger that produces the ~29 V main system bus from a 9–32 V input and manages multi-cell battery charging/balancing.
typical_failure_modes: R-2 board balancing/charging error requiring component-level rework modification on balance path
no ~29 V main bus output if input rail (VIN at R49/J1) is absent or F1 is blown
oscillating output if feedback network has cold joints or damaged passives
entries.notes: For R-2 boards with uneven cell balancing, consult MNT April 2021 R-2 motherboard update for the specific component modification. Confirm VIN at R49 before suspecting LTC4020. Source: community.mnt.re/t/mnt-reform-r-2-motherboard-update-2021-04-19/177

entries.role: Linear Technology LTC6803 multicell battery stack monitor that measures individual LiFePO4 cell voltages and reports data to U18 (System Controller) over SPI.
typical_failure_modes: incorrect balancing data reported to U18 if SPI signal integrity is poor
cell overvoltage/undervoltage not detected if LTC6803 loses power or SPI link
entries.notes: If balancing data appears wrong on R-2 boards, first verify SPI signal integrity between LTC6803 and U18. Source: community.mnt.re/t/mnt-reform-r-2-motherboard-update-2021-04-19/177

entries.role: Main downstream buck regulators on the v3.0 motherboard replacing older 5 V and 3.3 V converters; monitored via D12 (5 V) and D11 (3.3 V) indicator LEDs.
typical_failure_modes: low or oscillating 5 V / 3.3 V rail after SOM swap — regulator hiccup from incorrect sequencing
cold joint on output inductor or feedback resistors causing rail instability
increased output ripple if v3.0 output capacitors have cold joints
entries.notes: Scope the LM62460 feedback pin for instability if rail is low or oscillating. Check output capacitance — v3.0 board increased capacitance on 5 V USB rails. Source: MNT Reform Handbook hardware.html

entries.role: SMD input fuse in series on the barrel-jack input rail before R49; blown F1 leaves the board completely dead with no rail activity.
typical_failure_modes: open (blown) fuse after over-current or reverse-polarity event — board completely dead
intermittent continuity from cracked SMD fuse body after mechanical stress
entries.notes: Check F1 with DMM in diode-mode or resistance mode; if open, replace SMD fuse. First probe R49 for input voltage (9–32 V nominal 24 V) to confirm F1 is the fault. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Sense resistor on the main power-input path used as the primary test point for confirming input voltage (9–32 V) after the barrel jack.
typical_failure_modes: open resistor causing no voltage downstream of F1
lifted pad from mechanical stress on input connector area
entries.notes: Probe R49 with DMM for input voltage; absence indicates blown F1 or no input supply from J1. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Barrel-jack input connector accepting 9–32 V (nominal 24 V) supply; first point of entry for external power.
typical_failure_modes: pad lift or broken solder joint from mechanical stress on barrel connector
intermittent power from worn barrel socket contacts
entries.notes: Inspect J1 under microscope for lifted pads; re-flow with flux if joints appear cracked. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Debug/UART connector providing access to LPC_VCC (3.3 V, pin 15) and System Controller UART for power-on/off commands and diagnostics.
typical_failure_modes: no UART communication if LPC_VCC absent at pin 15
intermittent connection from cold joint on J22 pins
entries.notes: Measure LPC_VCC at J22 pin 15 (nominal 3.3 V) as first diagnostic step when System Controller is unresponsive. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: 30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch ZIF flex connector carrying MIPI-DSI signals from the CPU SOM to U10 (SN65DSI86); a critical signal-integrity link for the internal display.
typical_failure_modes: pad lift on ZIF pads after reassembly causing intermittent or blank display
damaged flex cable contacts from repeated re-seating
intermittent display flicker from mis-seated connector — no rail fault present
entries.notes: Inspect J24 ZIF pads under stereo microscope after any reassembly; re-seat or re-flow lifted contacts. Route Wi-Fi antenna away from DSI flex cable run to avoid signal interference. Pitch is 0.5 mm — use fine-tip iron and flux. Source: community.mnt.re/t/edp-cable-or-board-issue/3898

entries.role: White LED indicator that illuminates when LPC_VCC (3.3 V always-on rail) is live; primary visual diagnostic for System Controller power status.
typical_failure_modes: LED dark when LPC_VCC is absent — U14 or its supply has failed
LED extinguishes on `1P` command — suspect short on 3.3 V switched rail pulling down LPC_VCC
entries.notes: D11 off = LPC_VCC absent; probe U14 output and J22 pin 15. D11 lighting then extinguishing on power-on command suggests downstream short-to-GND on 3.3 V switched rail. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: LED indicator that illuminates when USB_PWR (5 V switched rail) is active; used to confirm the 5 V rail is enabled by the System Controller.
typical_failure_modes: LED stays dark after `1P` command — 5 V switching FETs not enabled by U18, or 5 V rail shorted
entries.notes: If D11 is lit but D12 is dark after power-on command, trace 5 V path from LTC4020 through switching FETs controlled by U18. Source: community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970

entries.role: Decoupling capacitor on the 1.8 V (VCC_1V8) rail; used as the primary DMM test point for confirming the 1.8 V rail is live.
typical_failure_modes: shorted MLCC pulling VCC_1V8 to GND (cracked cap from board flex)
open capacitor causing increased ripple on 1.8 V rail
entries.notes: Probe C130 in diode-mode to check for short before powering on. If 1.8 V is absent, diode-mode sweep nearby decoupling caps to isolate shorted cap. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Decoupling capacitor on the 1.2 V (VCC_1V2) rail; used as the primary DMM test point for confirming the 1.2 V rail powering U9 (TUSB8041) and U10 (SN65DSI86).
typical_failure_modes: shorted MLCC pulling VCC_1V2 to GND (cracked cap from board flex or thermal stress)
open capacitor causing increased ripple on 1.2 V rail
entries.notes: Probe C37 in diode-mode first; if shorted, systematically lift-test nearby caps to find the offending one. Source: MNT Reform Handbook advanced.html

entries.role: Micro-HDMI output connector on the Pocket Reform providing external video; susceptible to pad lift under repeated plug/unplug mechanical stress.
typical_failure_modes: pad lift on PCB pads under connector from repeated plug/unplug cycles
torn trace stubs requiring 30 AWG jumper wires to connector pins
cold joint on connector pins cracking after mechanical stress
entries.notes: Under stereo microscope, clean lifted pads and re-flow with flux and fine-tip iron. If pads are torn, scrape clearance coat from exposed trace stubs and run 30 AWG jumpers to connector pins. Source: community.mnt.re/t/tragedy-microhdmi-port-has-detached/4240
